title: Linguistics and language
explanation:
Linguistics and language

  • syntax, semantics, dialect, fluency, corpus
  • pronunciation, register, bilingualism, acquisition, utterance
  • verbs: articulate, translate, interpret, coin

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The course examines how ____ shapes sentence structure. | *syntax | symptom | system
She speaks with remarkable ____ in three languages. | *fluency | frequency | fluids
Researchers analysed a large ____ of spoken English. | *corpus | campus | census
Formal ____ differs from casual conversation. | *register | receipt | refund

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option. Use each option ONLY ONCE.

questions.text: Children acquire grammar through gradual language ____.  | acquisition
The interpreter had to ____ a highly technical speech. | interpret
He struggled to ____ his ideas under pressure. | articulate
Shakespeare helped ____ hundreds of new words. | coin
The ____ of the phrase changed over centuries. | meaning

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: ____ studies how words combine to create sense. | *Semantics | Sematic | Semantician
Regional ____ can vary sharply within one country. | *dialects | dialecting | dialected
Early ____ may give children cognitive advantages. | *bilingualism | bilinguality | bilinguist
Clear ____ helps learners be understood. | *pronunciation | pronounce | pronounced
